format_discipline.notes: H1 is first non-empty line. Sidecar parses cleanly. All six required sections present in correct order.

length.notes: 1335 words within 1200–1600 target. 1335/200 = 6.68 min, within 6–8 min target.

citations.notes: All three primary resources cited inline and in bibliography. No orphans.

no_forward_references.notes: Embeddings, vector databases, similarity search, chunking all deferred to Week 14 in correct bare-name + pointer form (lines 29, 113). No other forward references found.

faithfulness.notes: 11/12 sampled assertions trace directly to corpus. One borderline: the specific Sona Masoori rice product/price detail in the Worked Example is not in the corpus verbatim, but the customer-support chatbot / product-catalogue scenario is corpus-grounded (corpus §7.2). The specific product name and price are a plausible concretisation of a corpus-described scenario — not a factual fabrication. Count: 1, below the fail threshold of >2.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms (RAG, knowledge cutoff, query, knowledge source, enriched prompt) bolded and defined at first use. Hallucination cross-referenced to prior topic 4.2 per prerequisites. Deferred terms (embeddings, vector databases, similarity search) named-and-deferred, not defined. No cold unexplained acronyms. No hedging phrases. Register is plain, active, second-person throughout.

unlisted_sequence.description: The three RAG pipeline steps in the Key Concepts section are rendered as bold-headed sub-paragraphs (Step 1 —, Step 2 —, Step 3 —) rather than a numbered list as required by formatting_standards for procedures/sequences.

formatting.notes: Diagram embedded at line 22 with alt-text and caption. All definitions use bold-term pattern. Worked Example uses correctly numbered list. One soft finding: pipeline steps use headed paragraphs instead of numbered list.

assessment_reference.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights mentioned anywhere in the reading.

scope.notes: All content is within the topic headline. Fine-tuning referenced as prior topic 4.2 (appropriate). Advanced RAG components correctly named-and-deferred to Week 14 without bolding or defining. No scope_creep found.

soft_findings.description: Three RAG pipeline steps in Key Concepts use bold-headed sub-paragraphs instead of a numbered list.

gold_example_comparison.notes: Matches gold standard moves: bold-term-then-definition, concrete worked example in numbered steps, diagram embedded with caption, Key Takeaways as self-contained bullets. Does not replicate the gold example's assessment_reference defect (A1 line). 4.3 reading meets or exceeds the gold bar.

summary: Reading passes all hard checks. One soft finding (unlisted_sequence: pipeline steps in Key Concepts use headed paragraphs instead of a numbered list). Soft finding count (1) is below the escalation threshold (3). No scope_creep. No assessment references. Diagram correctly embedded. Word count in range. All corpus LOs addressed. Recommend PASS at HITL #3 with soft finding noted for author awareness.
